name: Davos Forum Full
description: The 'Davos Forum Full' is a conspiracy theory surrounding the World Economic Forum (WEF), an international organization based in Cologny, Geneva Canton, Switzerland. Proponents believe the WEF, through its annual meeting in Davos, Switzerland, and its associated initiatives (like the 'Great Reset'), is a clandestine organization aiming to establish a global totalitarian regime. They claim the WEF's elite members, comprised of global business leaders, politicians, academics, and journalists, are secretly planning to dismantle national sovereignty, abolish private property, implement a one-world government, and control the world's population through technological means (e.g., digital IDs, social credit systems). The origin of this theory can be traced back to anxieties about globalization, technological advancements, and perceived erosion of national identities. Key evidence cited includes WEF publications and statements, particularly Klaus Schwab's book 'COVID-19: The Great Reset,' which proponents interpret as a blueprint for a global power grab. Mainstream scholarly perspective dismisses these claims as unsubstantiated conspiracy theories. Academics and journalists argue that the WEF is a platform for discussion and collaboration, not a secretive cabal. They emphasize that the WEF's proposals are non-binding and subject to democratic processes within individual nations. Critics also point out the lack of concrete evidence supporting the claim of a coordinated global conspiracy and highlight the diverse range of opinions and interests represented within the WEF. The theory often incorporates anti-globalist sentiments, distrust of elites, and fears about technological surveillance.
subtitle: Global Elite Conspiracy Theory

claims.claim: The WEF aims to establish a one-world government.
claims.evidence: WEF's focus on global governance and collaboration.
claims.criticism: WEF promotes dialogue, not enforced governance; national sovereignty remains intact.

claims.claim: The 'Great Reset' is a plan for a totalitarian takeover.
claims.evidence: Klaus Schwab's book advocating for systemic changes.
claims.criticism: The 'Great Reset' is a proposal for addressing global challenges, not a mandatory plan.

claims.claim: The WEF seeks to abolish private property.
claims.evidence: Statements about stakeholder capitalism and a future 'where you'll own nothing and be happy'.
claims.criticism: WEF advocates for a more inclusive economic system, not the abolition of private property; the quote is often taken out of context and refers to a hypothetical future scenario, not a concrete policy.

claims.claim: The WEF uses COVID-19 as an opportunity to implement its agenda.
claims.evidence: Timing of the 'Great Reset' announcement during the pandemic.
claims.criticism: The pandemic highlighted existing global challenges, prompting discussions about potential solutions; the timing is coincidental, not causal.

keyFigures.name: Klaus Schwab
keyFigures.role: Founder and Executive Chairman of the WEF (Perceived antagonist)

keyFigures.name: Naomi Klein
keyFigures.role: Author critical of globalization and corporate power (Potential proponent, indirectly)

keyFigures.name: David Rothkopf
keyFigures.role: Author and commentator, critic of conspiracy theories surrounding the WEF

mythologyConnections: The Tower of Babel (representing hubris and a unified world)
Atlantis (a technologically advanced society brought down by its own ambition)

timeline.event: Founding of the European Management Forum (later World Economic Forum) by Klaus Schwab
Emergence of New World Order conspiracy theories, some linking to Davos
Announcement of the 'Great Reset' initiative, fueling conspiracy theories
Continued proliferation of Davos Forum conspiracy theories online

sources.author: David Rothkopf
sources.work: Superclass: Who Rules the World and How?
sources.title: David Rothkopf

sources.author: Sharan Burrow
sources.perspective: critic (of potential negative impacts of the Great Reset)
sources.title: Sharan Burrow
